title: Bytes, Unicode, Newlines, Front Matter, Metadata, and Schemas · Program Brief: Metadata Schema
summary: The build relies on locale encoding, silently replaces invalid bytes, parses arbitrary YAML objects, accepts duplicate metadata keys, and compares dates in mixed formats. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the metadata schema increment in versioned decoded-document and metadata contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, chang…
objectives: Validate allowed keys, required values, types, formats, unknown fields, duplicates, defaults, and source locations.
Recognize bounded delimiters, separate metadata bytes from body, reject malformed closure, and avoid executing content.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Metadata Schema · Theory Metadata Schema

Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ssg-metadata-schema — Validate allowed keys, required values, types, formats, unknown fields, duplicates, defaults, and source locations.
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-text-encoding-metadata-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-text-encoding-metadata-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_text_encoding_metadata_4(byte_count: int = 240, decoded_units: int = 228, metadata_fields: int = 6, decode_errors: int = 0, schema_errors: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if byte_count < 0 or decoded_units < 0:
        return False, decoded_units, "text-count"
    if decoded_units > byte_count:
        return False, decoded_units, "encoding-units"
    if metadata_fields < 3:
        return False, metadata_fields, "metadata-shape"
    if decode_errors > 0 or schema_errors > 0:
        return False, decode_errors + schema_errors, "invalid-document"
    assert byte_count >= decoded_units
    return True, metadata_fields, "validated-document"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Metadata Schema model · Theory Metadata Schema
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Metadata Schema: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 6146 during a large technical reference incremental rebuild. Build deterministic Python evidence to decode UTF-8 content and validate bounded front matter without hiding data loss; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then replace a trusted author fixture with untrusted raw HTML, unsafe URLs, and an active SVG. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Validate allowed keys, required values, types, formats, unknown fields, duplicates, defaults, and source locations.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Metadata Schema model · Theory Metadata Schema.
